text:
Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

1.101 SEVERE ALLERGIC REACTION
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 2/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 1 of 2 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To rapidly treat a severe allergic reaction.

ITEMS:
Gray Tape
Emergency Medical Treatment Pack (Red):
Epinephrine (EpiPen)

1. DEPLOYING AND USING EPINEPHRINE (EPIPEN)
1.1 Remove Epinephrine (EpiPen) from protective plastic tubing.
(Figure 1)

[Figure 1]

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows an Epinephrine (EpiPen) and its protective plastic tubing. The image contains two main objects: a clear plastic tube with a yellow cap at one end, labeled "Protective Plastic Tubing," and the auto-injector device itself. The auto-injector is a white cylindrical device with a blue "Safety Cap" at one end and an orange "Needle End" at the opposite end. The image serves to identify the components of the Epinephrine (EpiPen) and its storage tubing as part

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

(EpiPen) and its storage tubing as part of the deployment and use procedure for treating a severe allergic reaction.

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

Figure 1.- Epinephrine (EpiPen).

1.2 Form a fist around Epinephrine (EpiPen) with thumb closest to safety cap.

1.3 Activate auto injector by pulling off safety cap.
(Figure 2)

WARNING
1. Do not put fingers over needle end of Epinephrine (EpiPen) to
prevent injection into finger.
2. Do not give shot through clothing to prevent needle contamination.

23 MAR 15
1.101_M_22808.doc

1.101 SEVERE ALLERGIC REACTION
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 2/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 2 of 2 pages

[Figure 2]

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

[Figure 2]

> **[Figure description]** A person's hands are shown preparing an Epinephrine (EpiPen) auto-injector. The left hand is closed in a fist around the body of the device, while the right hand's thumb and index finger are pulling a blue safety cap away from the top of the injector. The device has an orange end and a clear plastic section. This image illustrates step 1.3 of the procedure for activating the auto-injector by removing the safety cap.

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

Figure 2.- Activating Epinephrine (EpiPen).

1.4 Using a jabbing motion, press the needle end perpendicular to and firmly
against patient's outer thigh until a click is heard or felt.
Hold firmly against thigh for 10 seconds.
(Figure 3)

[Figure 3]

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

> **[Figure description]** An illustration showing the correct positioning of an Epinephrine (EpiPen) auto-injector during administration. A person's hand is shown gripping the device in a fist and pressing the needle end perpendicular to and firmly against the outer thigh of a patient. The patient is seated on a wooden surface and wearing blue clothing. This image is labeled as Figure 3 and serves to demonstrate the physical application of the medication to the thigh as described in step 1.4 medication to the thigh as described in step 1.4 of the procedure.

Emergency Procedure: Severe Allergic Reaction

Figure 3.- Positioning of Epinephrine (EpiPen).

1.5 Discard used Epinephrine (EpiPen) in original protective plastic tubing;
secure in tube with Gray Tape and discard.
Call MCC-H to request PMC.

1.6 Epinephrine (EpiPen) may be repeated every 20 minutes if symptoms recur
or progress.

2. Go to 2.0.403 ALLERGIC REACTION, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS,
PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT).

23 MAR 15
1.101_M_22808.doc

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

MED C/L

1.102 AED ASSISTED CPR
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 1 of 1 page

Updated via P&I in 44-0397

If one rescuer available -> 1 ASSESS

If two rescuers available
Rescuer 1 -> 1 ASSESS
Rescuer 2 -> Deploy AED, then: Go to 1.103 ALS ALGORITHIM, block 1 (SODF: MED CL: EMERGENCY)

1 ASSESS
1. Assess environment for safety hazards.
2. Assess patient status.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

-> Patient unresponsive and breathing normally -> 2 
-> Patient unresponsive and not breathing or no normal breathing (only gasping) -> 3


2 TRANSPORT / PROVIDE CARE
1. Relocate to and restrain patient on the Crew Medical Restraint System.
2. Page additional crewmember for assistance.
3. Call MCC-H to request PMC.
4. Go to 1.103 ALS ALGORITHM, block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment (SODF: MED CL: EMERGENCY).

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

3 PERFORM CPR
Perform 30 chest compressions for every 2 breaths (1 second each breath) for 5 cycles (approximately 2 minutes).
1. As required, relocate to and restrain patient on the Crew Medical Restraint System.
2. Page additional crewmember for assistance.
3. Call MCC-H to request PMC. -> 4

4 DEPLOY AED
1. Connect AED electrodes to patient's chest. (Figure 1)
2. AED 'ON' (green) -> Press
3. Follow verbal prompts.
4. If verbal prompts inaudible, read prompts on screen. -> 5

[Figure 1]

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

[Figure 1]

> **[Figure description]** This image is a figure from a medical procedure document illustrating the placement of Automated External Defibrillator (AED) electrodes on a human torso.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

The image consists of a procedural flowchart and a corresponding diagram. The flowchart contains two numbered sections:
- Section 1 includes steps to relocate and restrain the patient on the Crew Medical Restraint System, page an additional crewmember for assistance, and call MCC-H to request PMC.
- Section 4, titled "DEPLOY AED," lists steps to connect AED electrodes to the patient's chest (referencing Figure 1), press the green 'ON' button, and follow verbal or screen prompts.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

Figure 1 is a line drawing of a human torso from the waist up. It shows the correct placement of two AED electrode pads: one pad is placed on the upper right chest, and the second pad is placed on the lower left side of the chest, below the armpit. A cable is shown extending from the pads toward the top right of the frame.

The image serves as a visual guide for the "DEPLOY AED" step of the medical procedure, specifically demonstrating the anatomical positioning of the electrodes.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

Figure 1.- AED Electrode Placement.

5 MONITOR PATIENT RESPONSE
After AED prompts 'Start CPR'
Perform a 10-second pulse check.
Is pulse detected?
-> Yes -> 6 
-> No -> 7

6 ASSIST PATIENT
If patient begins spontaneous breathing or a pulse is detected
Stop chest compressions.
Assist breathing as necessary.
If second rescuer assisting
Stop medication administration.
Go to 1.103 ALS ALGORITHM, block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment (SODF: MED CL: EMERGENCY).

Emergency Procedure: Aed Assisted Cpr

7 PERFORM CPR
Perform 30 chest compressions for every 2 breaths (1 second each breath) until prompted by AED.
Repeat block 5 after each 'Start CPR' prompt from AED.

09 APR 15
1.102 M_22809.doc

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 1 of 7 pages

INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE INSERTION

If two rescuers available
Rescuer 2 -> 1
Rescuer 1 -> Go to 1.102 AED ASSISTED CPR, all (SODF: MED CL: EMERGENCY)

If pulse returns, stop chest compressions and medication administration. Give breaths and go to block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

-> 1 PREPARE INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE
1. Unstow
Emergency Medical Treatment Pack (Red):
Scissors - Trauma
Intraosseous Device Start Kit:
Intraosseous Injection Device
IV Cap
Medical Tape
Syringe 10 mL
2. Tear off two 10 cm pieces of Medical Tape and set aside.
3. Attach IV Cap to Syringe. (Figure 1)
4. Expose leg to knee [Scissors - Trauma]. 
-> 2


[Figure 1]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows a Syringe IV Cap Assembly. The image displays a clear plastic 10 mL syringe with black measurement markings and a "B-D" brand label near the base. A blue IV cap is attached to the tip of the syringe. The entire assembly is positioned vertically against a white, textured fabric background. This assembly is prepared in step 3 of the "PREPARE INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE" section and is later used in step 4 of "POST INSERTION STEPS" to clear bone fragments from an INSERTION STEPS" to clear bone fragments from an intraosseous needle.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 1.- Syringe IV Cap Assembly.


2 IDENTIFY INTRAOSSEOUS SITE
1. Place finger on bony protrusion identified by circle. (Figure 2)
2. Move finger 2 cm towards the inner leg.
3. Move finger 1 cm up towards knee, identified by "+". (Figure 2)
-> 3


[Figure 2]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

> **[Figure description]** Figure 2 illustrates the identification of the intraosseous site on a human leg. The image shows a close-up of a leg with three key markers: a label and arrow pointing to the "Kneecap," a label and arrow pointing to a "Bony protrusion" that is circled on the skin, and a black plus sign ("+") located above and to the left of the circled bony protrusion. The image serves as a visual guide for locating the specific insertion point for an intraosseous device relative to point for an intraosseous device relative to these anatomical landmarks.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 2.- Intraosseous Site.


MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 2 of 7 pages

If pulse returns, stop chest compressions and medication administration. Give breaths and go to block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment.

3 POSITION INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE
WARNING
Needle could eject from end of blue barrel becoming a projectile
1. Remove Intraosseous Device from packaging and place blue barrel on insertion point. (Figure 3) 
-> 4

[Figure 3]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

[Figure 3]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows a close-up of a syringe and IV cap assembly. A person's hand is holding a white syringe, which is attached to a blue IV cap. The assembly is positioned over a person's leg, where a small circle has been drawn on the skin. The image illustrates the preparation of the syringe and IV cap assembly as part of the "PREPARE INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE" step in the intraosseous device insertion procedure.

Figure 3.- Positioning Intraosseous Device.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 3.- Positioning Intraosseous Device.


4 ACTIVATE INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE
1. Remove safety latch and stow for later use.
2. Maintain positive pressure of blue barrel perpendicular to skin at insertion point. (Figure 3)
3. Place palm of other hand over the gray end of Intraosseous Device and apply gentle pressure to activate. A click will sound as the device activates. (Figure 4)
4. Gently lift Intraosseous Device, exposing the needle unit. 
-> 5


[Figure 4]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

[Figure 4]

> **[Figure description]** A close-up photograph showing a person's hands activating an intraosseous device on a patient's leg. One hand holds the blue barrel of the device perpendicular to the skin, while the palm of the other hand is placed over the gray end of the device, applying pressure to activate it. The device is positioned on the upper leg. This image is labeled as Figure 4 and illustrates the "Activating Intraosseous Device" step of the procedure. Figure 4.- Activating Intraosseous Device.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

5 POST INSERTION STEPS
1. Place safety latch between skin and hub of Intraosseous needle. (Figure 5)
2. Place Medical Tape over safety latch to secure. (Figure 6)
3. Stabilize Intraosseous needle with hand; with other hand, remove inner needle.
4. Remove sterile cover from IV Cap, then attach Syringe and IV Cap assembly to Intraosseous needle. (Figure 6)

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

5. Pull back on plunger of Syringe >= 5 mL, then disconnect Syringe from blue IV Cap. Blood or fluid return may or may not be seen. This action will potentially clear bone fragments from the needle. (Figure 7) 
-> 6 [Figure 5]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

> **[Figure description]** A close-up photograph showing a person's hand identifying an intraosseous insertion site on a patient's leg. A finger is placed on a specific point on the skin, marked with a small "+" symbol. To the right of the finger, a black circle is drawn on the skin, with a dotted line connecting the circle to the "+" mark. A red plastic tool or guide is positioned horizontally across the skin, overlapping the "+" mark. This image is labeled as Figure 2 and serves to illustrate

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

is labeled as Figure 2 and serves to illustrate the process of identifying the correct intraosseous site based on anatomical landmarks. Figure 5.- Intraosseous Needle with Safety Latch in Place.
[Figure 6]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows a close-up view of a person's hands assembling a syringe and an IV cap. One hand holds a clear plastic syringe vertically, while the other hand guides a blue IV cap as it is attached to the tip of the syringe. The assembly is being performed over a patient's skin, where two pieces of white medical tape are visible. This image illustrates the "Syringe IV Cap Assembly" step of the Intraosseous Device Insertion procedure.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 6.- Syringe Attached to Intraosseous Needle.
[Figure 7]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows a Syringe IV Cap Assembly. The image displays a blue-capped syringe attached to a small connector, which is resting on two white pieces of medical tape. The assembly is positioned centrally on a neutral-colored surface. This image illustrates the preparation step of attaching the IV Cap to the syringe before the intraosseous device is inserted.

Figure 7.- Intraosseous Needle, Secured.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 7.- Intraosseous Needle, Secured.


MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 3 of 7 pages

If pulse returns, stop chest compressions and medication administration. Give breaths and go to block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

6 ADMINISTER EPINEPHRINE
1. Retrieve Epinephrine from ALS Medication Kit [Emergency Medical Treatment Pack (Red)].
2. Prepare one Epinephrine Syringe. (Figure 8)
3. Attach to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle.
4. Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle.
-> 7

[Figure 8]

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

[Figure 8]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1 shows the components for the Syringe IV Cap Assembly. The image displays a 10 mL syringe with a green tip and a plunger extended, a smaller syringe with a red marking on the barrel and a gray cap, and several yellow plastic caps of varying sizes. The items are laid out on a white fabric surface. This image serves as a visual reference for preparing the syringe and IV cap assembly used during the intraosseous device insertion procedure.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Figure 8.- Epinephrine Syringes.


7 Did the medicine flow through the Intraosseous needle?
-> Yes -> 9
-> No -> 8

8 Go to block 20, page 7, Intraosseous Needle Troubleshooting.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

9 ADMINISTER ATROPINE OR LIDOCAINE
Wait for next AED rhythm analysis.
1. If no shock advised, administer Atropine.
(a) Prepare one Atropine Syringe [ALS Medication Kit].
(b) Attach Syringe to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle.
(c) Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle. -> 10
2. If shock advised, administer Lidocaine.
(a) Prepare one Lidocaine Syringe [ALS Medication Kit].
(b) Attach Syringe to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle. (c) Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle.
-> 10

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

10 ADMINISTER EPINEPHRINE
Wait for next AED rhythm analysis.
1. Prepare one Epinephrine Syringe [ALS Medication Kit].
2. Attach to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle.
3. Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle. 
-> 11

MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 4 of 7 pages

If pulse returns, stop chest compressions and medication administration. Give breaths and go to block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment.

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

11 ADMINISTER ATROPINE OR LIDOCAINE
Wait for next AED rhythm analysis.
1. If no shock advised, administer Atropine.
(a) Prepare one Atropine Syringe [ALS Medication Kit].
(b) Attach Syringe to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle.
(c) Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle. -> 12
2. If shock advised, administer Lidocaine.
(a) Prepare one Lidocaine Syringe [ALS Medication Kit].
(b) Attach Syringe to IV Cap on Intraosseous needle. (c) Administer contents of Syringe into Intraosseous needle.
-> 12

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

12 CPR TERMINATION
1. Coordinate with Surgeon, if available.
2. Consider CPR termination if all of the following conditions apply:
At least 10 minutes of CPR since last dose of medication, and
No pulse or spontaneous breathing, and
At least 45 minutes elapsed since start of resuscitation.

MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 5 of 7 pages

RESPIRATORY ASSESSMENT

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

RESPIRATORY ASSESSMENT

If AED advises 'PUSH SHOCK BUTTON', or pulse is not detected, follow AED prompts and go to block 1, page 1, Intraosseous Device Insertion.

13 CHECK PATIENT RESPONSE
Is patient awake and able to speak?
-> Yes -> 15
-> No -> 14

14 ASSESS BREATHING
Are any of the following true?
Skin of lips, face, or hands blue/gray.
Breathing rate < 12 or > 30 breaths per minute.
Patient snoring or having difficulty inhaling.
-> No to all -> 15
-> Yes to any -> 16

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

-> 15 PROVIDE OXYGEN WITH MASK
1. Unstow Respiratory Support Pack
2. Locate Cue Card in lid pocket.
3. Provide oxygen by performing 3.201 RESPIRATORY SUPPORT PACK CUE CARD, Oxygen Via Low Flow Non Rebreather Mask (SODF: MED CL: REFERENCE), then:
-> 17

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

16 ASSIST BREATHING AND PROVIDE OXYGEN USING AMBU BAG
1. Assist breathing using mouth to mouth breaths or AMBU Bag. (Administer breaths at a rate of one breath every 5 seconds).
2. Unstow Respiratory Support Pack
3. Locate Cue Card in lid pocket.
4. Provide oxygen using AMBU Bag by performing 3.201 RESPIRATORY SUPPORT PACK - CUE CARD, Oxygen Via AMBU Bag (SODF: MED CL: REFERENCE), then:
-> 17

MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 6 of 7 pages

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

If AED advises 'PUSH SHOCK BUTTON', or pulse is not detected, follow AED prompts and go to block 1, page 1, Intraosseous Device Insertion.

17 LUNG ASSESSMENT
1. Retrieve Stethoscope [Emergency Medical Treatment Pack (Red)].
2. Verify presence of breath sounds on both sides of the chest [Stethoscope].
-> 18

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

18 ADMINISTER IV FLUIDS
If Intraosseous Device is inserted
Give IV fluids via Intraosseous needle by performing 2.14.306 INJECTIONS & IV - GIVING IV FLUIDS AND MEDICATIONS, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT), then:
If Intraosseous Device is not inserted
Insert Intraosseous Device by performing blocks 1 to 5, pages 1 and 2, then:

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

Give fluids via Intraosseous needle by performing 2.14.306 INJECTIONS & IV - GIVING IV FLUIDS AND MEDICATIONS, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT), then:
-> 19

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

19 ATTACH MONITORING EQUIPMENT
1. Unstow BP/ECG Medical Diagnostic Pack (Blue): Blood Oximeter
2. Insert four AA Batteries into Blood Oximeter and attach to patient.
3. Perform 2.7.301 CHEST PROCEDURE - OBTAIN ELECTROCARDIOGRAM (ECG), all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT), then:
4. Call MCC-H to request PMC.

MED C/L

1.103 ALS ALGORITHM
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T) Page 7 of 7 pages

INTRAOSSEOUS NEEDLE TROUBLESHOOTING

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

INTRAOSSEOUS NEEDLE TROUBLESHOOTING

If pulse returns, stop chest compressions and medication administration. Give breaths and go to block 13, page 5, Respiratory Assessment.

20
Have two attempts at placing the Intraosseous device been made?
-> Yes -> 21
-> No -> 22

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

21
1. Attach Syringe 10 mL to IV Cap on failed Intraosseous Device(s).
2. Pull back on plunger of Syringe 10 mL >= 5 mL, then disconnect Syringe 10 mL from blue IV Cap. Blood or fluid may or may not be seen. This action will potentially clear bone fragments from the needle.
-> 24

22 PREPARE FOR SECOND INTRAOSSEOUS DEVICE INSERTION USING OTHER LEG
Retrieve Intraosseous Device Start Kit with Second Intraosseous Device [Emergency Medical Treatment Pack (Red)].
-> 23

Emergency Procedure: Als Algorithm

23
1. Go to block 1, page 1, Intraosseous Device Insertion, and perform steps using other leg.

24
Attempt to re-administer medication.
1. If successful, go to block 9, page 3.
2. If unsuccessful, Support AED Assisted CPR and report results to Surgeon, if able.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting

1.104 AED TROUBLESHOOTING
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 1/Paper on ISS/T) Page 1 of 2 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To identify problem conditions that may be encountered during use of the AED, and provide possible solutions.

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting | Observation | Possible Cause | Action | |---|---|---| | CONNECT ELECTRODES voice prompt is heard | Poor AED Electrode-to-skin contact | 1. Firmly press AED Electrodes on patient's skin.
2. If this does not correct problem and large amount of chest hair present Swiftly remove AED Electrodes and any attached chest hair.
3. Place new set of AED Electrodes on chest. | | | AED Electrodes are dry or damaged | Replace the AED Electrode pads. |

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting

| | AED Electrodes are not removed from the liner | Remove the AED Electrodes from the liner and apply them to the patient's chest. |
| CHECK CONNECTOR AND ELECTRODES voice prompt is heard | Connection to the AED is inadequate | Check to be sure that the AED Electrode connector is inserted completely. |
| MOTION DETECTED and STOP MOTION voice prompts are heard | Patient movement because of breathing | Check patient for normal breathing. |

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting | | CPR being performed during analysis | Stop CPR during analysis. | | | Electrical/radio frequency interference | Move suspected devices away from the AED when possible. | | STATUS DISPLAY is blank | The AED has been turned on | Normal condition when AED is in use | | MAIN DISPLAY blank and ON LED lit | LCD not operating properly
MAIN DISPLAY not functioning properly | 1. AED may still operate. If needed, continue to use device to treat patient.
2. Notify MCC-H. |

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting

17 MAR 15
1.104 M_22811.doc

1.104 AED TROUBLESHOOTING
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 1/Paper on ISS/T) Page 2 of 2 pages | Observation | Possible Cause | Action |
|---|---|---|

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting | AED does not deliver visual prompts, voice prompts, or auditory tones after it is powered on, or the voice prompts sound faint or distorted | Low AED Battery (one bar or less left in MAIN DISPLAY) | 1. Continue CPR if patient is not responding, not breathing normally, and showing no signs of circulation.
2. Check Battery indicator in MAIN DISPLAY.
3. Replace AED Battery at earliest opportunity.
4. Notify MCC-H.

NOTE
AED can deliver 30 shocks and 75 minutes of monitoring if deliver 30 shocks and 75 minutes of monitoring if Battery shows one bar in the MAIN DISPLAY. |

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting | REPLACE BATTERY voice prompt is heard or visual prompt is seen | Low AED Battery (one bar or less left in MAIN DISPLAY) | 1. Continue CPR if patient is not responding, not breathing normally, and showing no signs of circulation.
2. Check Battery indicator in MAIN DISPLAY.
3. Replace AED Battery at earliest opportunity.
4. Notify MCC-H. |

Emergency Procedure: Aed Troubleshooting | AED cannot deliver the required shock
Flashing STATUS DISPLAY | Depleted AED Battery | 1. Continue CPR if patient is not responding, not breathing normally, and showing no signs of circulation.
2. Replace AED Battery.
3. Notify MCC-H. | 17 MAR 15 1.104 M_22811.doc

Emergency Procedure: Choking

MED C/L
Patient Choking
20 APR 15
1.105 CHOKING
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 2/Paper on ISS/T) Page 1 of 1 page

OBJECTIVE:
To rapidly clear airway obstruction.

-> 1 CHECK PATIENT RESPONSE
Is patient awake?
-> YES -> 2
-> NO -> 3

Emergency Procedure: Choking

2 PERFORM ABDOMINAL THRUSTS
1. Position behind patient.
2. Make fist.
3. Place fist above belly button but below ribcage. (Figure 1)
4. Pull sharply inward and upward.
5. Repeat until object clears or patient becomes unconscious. 
-> PATIENT BECOMES UNCONSCIOUS -> 3
-> OBJECT CLEARS -> 4


[Figure 1]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1, titled "Abdominal Thrust," consists of three line drawings illustrating the correct hand placement for performing abdominal thrusts on a choking patient.

Emergency Procedure: Choking

The left drawing shows a person standing behind a patient, wrapping their arms around the patient's waist. 

The top-right drawing shows a skeletal view of a ribcage and torso. A hand is shown forming a fist and placing it on the abdomen, specifically in the area below the ribcage and above the belly button. 

The bottom-right drawing shows a close-up skeletal view of the lower ribcage and abdomen, depicting the second hand gripping the first fist to prepare for the inward and upward pull.

Emergency Procedure: Choking

Figure 1.- Abdominal Thrust.


3 CLEAR AIRWAY/GIVE BREATHS
1. Open airway using Head tilt-Chin lift. (Figure 2)
2. Inspect for foreign object and remove, if easily accessible.
3. Pinch nose and attempt to give breaths.
Go to 1.102 AED ASSISTED CPR, all (SODF: MED CL: EMERGENCY).

[Figure 2]

Emergency Procedure: Choking

> **[Figure description]** A medical illustration labeled "Figure 2.- Head Tilt-Chin Lift" demonstrating a technique to open a patient's airway. The image shows a person lying on their back with two hands performing the maneuver: one hand is placed on the forehead to tilt the head back, and the other hand is positioned under the chin to lift it upward. Red curved arrows indicate the direction of movement for the head tilt and chin lift. This figure illustrates step 1 of the "CLEAR AIRWAY/GIVE illustrates step 1 of the "CLEAR AIRWAY/GIVE BREATHS" section of the choking procedure.

Emergency Procedure: Choking

Figure 2.- Head Tilt-Chin Lift.

4 OBTAIN VITAL SIGNS
1. Unstow
Medical Diagnostic Pack (Blue):
Blood Pressure Cuff (select appropriate size)
Blood Pressure Monitor
2. Take pulse, respiratory rate, and blood pressure.
3. Record data for later reporting to Surgeon.
-> 5

5 CONSULT SURGEON
Call MCC-H to request PMC.

1.105_M_22812.doc

Emergency Procedure: Toxic Exposure

1.106 TOXIC EXPOSURE
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 1 of 2 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To decontaminate and treat after toxic exposure.

ITEMS:
Drink Bag
Towel

TOOLS:
Digital Camera

1. RESCUER SAFETY
To minimize the number of crew exposed, take respiratory, eye, and skin
precautions prior to removing patient from exposure.

Emergency Procedure: Toxic Exposure

2. DECONTAMINATING PATIENT
2.1 Skin Exposure
Quickly remove soiled clothes.
Brush off visible particulate and wipe liquids off patient with Towel.
Irrigate contaminated areas with water.

2.2 Eye Exposure
If wearing contact lenses
Remove and discard.

Emergency Procedure: Toxic Exposure

If eye exposure occurs
Immediately flush with water from Drink Bag.
Configure eyewash station in the SM at the CBO-3B port, then irrigate
eye until pain resolves or directed to stop by Surgeon.
If required, refer to 2.3.304 EYE PROCEDURE - EYEWASH KIT USE,
all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT).

2.3 Respiratory Exposure
If difficulty breathing
Perform 1.108 DIFFICULTY BREATHING, all (SODF: MED CL:
EMERGENCY PROCEDURES), then:

3. PERFORMING EXAM
3.1 Call MCC-H to request PMC.

Emergency Procedure: Toxic Exposure

11 JUN 15
1.106_M_22813.doc

1.106 TOXIC EXPOSURE
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 2 of 2 pages

Emergency Procedure: Seizure

1.107 SEIZURE
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 3/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 1 of 3 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To give medication to suppress seizure activity. Seizures will cause loss of consciousness and
uncontrolled muscle contraction.

ITEMS:
AA Batteries (four)
Respiratory Support Pack
Medical Diagnostic Pack (Blue):
Blood Oximeter
Oral Medication Pack (Purple):
Phenytoin (Dilantin ER)
Topical & Injectable Medication Pack (Brown):
Diazepam (Valium)

TOOLS:
Digital Camera

Emergency Procedure: Seizure

TOOLS:
Digital Camera

1. PROTECTING FROM INJURY
Protect patient from injury by preventing impact of the body with hard structures.
Attempt to locate the patient in the center of a module away from structures.

NOTE
1. Never attempt to pry open or put anything in the mouth during a seizure.

2. Seizures typically last less than a minute.

2. ADMINISTERING INTRAMUSCULAR SHOT

NOTE
Step 2 and step 3 may be performed in parallel if multiple CMOs are available.

Emergency Procedure: Seizure

2.1 Configure Medication for Injection
Prepare 2 mL Diazepam (Valium) 5 mg/mL for a total of 10 mg [Topical &
Injectable Medication Pack (Brown)].

Emergency Procedure: Difficulty Breathing

1.108 DIFFICULTY BREATHING
(MED CL/HTV5 - ALL/FIN 2/Paper on ISS/T)
Page 1 of 3 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To evaluate and start treatment for difficulty breathing.

ITEMS:
AA Batteries (four)
Respiratory Support Pack

Medical Diagnostic Pack (Blue):
Blood Oximeter
Blood Pressure Cuff (select appropriate size)
Blood Pressure Monitor
Stethoscope
Topical & Injectable Medication Pack (Brown):
Albuterol (Proventil HFA)

Emergency Procedure: Difficulty Breathing

1. STARTING TREATMENT
1.1 Count Pulse and Respiratory Rate
Measure each by counting for 15 seconds and multiplying by four.
Record data for later reporting to Surgeon.

1.2 Administer Oxygen
Retrieve Respiratory Support Pack.
Locate Cue Card in lid pocket.
Provide oxygen via Low Flow Non-Rebreather Mask.
Perform 3.201 RESPIRATORY SUPPORT PACK - CUE CARD, Oxygen via
Low Flow Non-Rebreather Mask (SODF: MED CL: REFERENCE), then:

Emergency Procedure: Difficulty Breathing

1.3 Administer Inhaled Albuterol (Proventil HFA)
Shake the Albuterol (Proventil HFA) Inhaler to mix medication and then
remove cap [Topical & Injectable Medication Pack (Brown)].
Have patient fully exhale and place inhaler in mouth.
Have patient begin to inhale slowly through the mouth and then activate the
inhaler.
Have patient hold breath for 8 to 10 seconds before exhaling.
Deliver a second dose.

1. IDENTIFYING ANATOMIC LANDMARKS

[Figure 1]

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

> **[Figure description]** An anatomical illustration of an open human mouth, labeled as Figure 1. Mouth Anatomy. The image identifies several key anatomic landmarks with leader lines pointing to specific body parts: "Teeth" at the top, "Tonsil" on the left side of the throat, "Uvula" hanging in the center of the throat, "Tongue" in the center foreground, and "Gingiva (gum)" at the bottom. The illustration shows the interior of the oral cavity, including the upper and lower dental arches and

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

including the upper and lower dental arches and the soft palate. This image serves to demonstrate mouth anatomy as part of a medical procedure document.

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

Figure 1. Mouth Anatomy.

[Figure 2]

> **[Figure description]** An anatomical model of human upper and lower jaws and teeth, used to demonstrate mouth anatomy and tooth numbering.

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

The top portion of the model shows the maxillary (upper) jaw, labeled "Maxillary Upper Horseshoe." The teeth are numbered 1 through 16. A central section is labeled "Anteriors 1x6" with the note "2 x 6 or 1 x 12 upper/lower." The side sections are labeled "Posteriors 1 x 8" with the note "2 x 8 or 1 x 16 upper/lower."

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

The bottom portion of the model shows the mandibular (lower) jaw, labeled "Mandibular Lower Lingual bar." The teeth are numbered 17 through 32. The front section is labeled "Anteriors" and the back section is labeled "Posteriors."

The image serves as a visual guide for identifying anatomic landmarks and the numbering system for teeth.


Figure 2. Tooth Numbering.

[Figure 3]

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

Figure 2. Tooth Numbering.

[Figure 3]

> **[Figure description]** A medical diagram illustrating the anatomy of a tooth, labeled as Figure 3 in the document. The image shows a cross-section of a tooth embedded in the jawbone.

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

The diagram includes the following labeled anatomical components:
- Enamel: The outermost white layer covering the crown of the tooth.
- Dentin: The layer beneath the enamel, making up the bulk of the tooth.
- Pulp: The central core of the tooth containing blood vessels and nerves.
- Cementum: The layer covering the root of the tooth.
- Periodontal Ligament: The thin tissue layer between the cementum and the jawbone. - Jawbone: The porous, yellow-brown bone structure surrounding the tooth root.

Dental Reference: Mouth Anatomy

The image serves to identify the internal and external anatomic landmarks of a tooth for medical training purposes.


Figure 3. Tooth Anatomy.

Nitrile Gloves (select appropriate size)

Lidocaine (Xylocaine)

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

1. PREPARING A WORKSPACE
Create workstation using Gray Tape and temporarily stow hardware on tape.
Use Ziplock Bag for trash stowage during procedure.

2. Place a Benzocaine Swabstick topical numbing stick on injection site [Topical &
Injectable Medication Pack (Brown)].
Leave in place for 5 to 10 minutes prior to injections.

3. CONFIGURING SYRINGE FOR DENTAL INJECTION

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

3. CONFIGURING SYRINGE FOR DENTAL INJECTION

NOTE
1. A standard Syringe 10 mL will be modified to allow for one-handed aspiration (or
pulling back on plunger).
2. Aspiration allows operator to make sure the needle is not in a blood vessel.

3.1 Tear off an 8-inch strip of Medical Tape [Medical Supply Pack (Green)].
Twist the middle of the tape around itself.
Pull plunger out of syringe.
Apply the ends of the tape to the plunger.
(Figure 1)

2.6.301 DENTAL PROCEDURE - NERVE BLOCK
(MED CL/SpX-6 - ALL/FIN 1/T)
Page 2 of 3 pages

[Figure 1]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 1, titled "Modification to Make Aspirating Syringe," consists of four panels showing the process of modifying a 10 mL syringe using medical tape.

The top-left panel shows a strip of white medical tape positioned above a 10 mL syringe. The top-right panel shows the strip of tape with its middle section twisted around itself.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

The bottom-left panel shows the modified syringe from a side view. The plunger has been removed from the barrel, and the ends of the twisted tape are attached to the plunger, creating a loop that extends outside the barrel.

The bottom-right panel provides a close-up view of the tape loop attached to the plunger and extending from the back of the syringe barrel.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Figure 1. Modification to Make Aspirating Syringe.

3.2 Draw up 10 mL of Lidocaine (Xylocaine) into Syringe 10 mL.
If required, refer to 2.14.303 INJECTIONS AND IV - DRAWING UP MEDICINE
FROM VIAL, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT).

4. PREPARING PATIENT
Don Nitrile Gloves and LED Headlamp for adequate lighting.
Pack Gauze 4"x4" between gum and teeth to assist in visualizing injection location.

5. SELECTING INJECTION LOCATION
If using upper nerve block
Go to step 5.1.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

If using lower nerve block
Go to step 5.2.

5.1 Upper Injection Technique
Needle 25G →|← Syringe 10 mL
Remove needle cap and temporarily stow on workspace.
Place needle at height of moveable membrane (muccobuccal fold) above fixed
gum tissue.
(Figure 2)

[Figure 2]

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

[Figure 2]

> **[Figure description]** A medical photograph showing the placement of a dental syringe for an upper nerve block. The image displays an open mouth with the upper and lower teeth visible. A dental syringe is positioned with the needle inserted into the upper gum area.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Two labels with arrows identify key components:
- "Moveable Membrane (mucobuccal fold)" points to the area of the upper gum tissue where the needle is inserted.
- "Dental Syringe" points to the syringe body and plunger.

The image serves as a visual guide for the "Upper Injection Technique," demonstrating the correct height and location for needle placement at the mucobuccal fold above the fixed gum tissue.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Figure 2. Upper Nerve Block.
Insert needle, directing needle toward root of tooth to be anesthetized.
Gently pull back on syringe plunger and inspect chamber to ensure no blood in
chamber.
Inject 2 to 3 mL of Lidocaine (Xylocaine) directly over the root of the tooth.
Withdraw needle; recap it using one-handed technique and temporarily stow.

15 APR 15
2.6.301_M_22895.xml

2.6.301 DENTAL PROCEDURE - NERVE BLOCK
(MED CL/SpX-6 - ALL/FIN 1/T)
Page 3 of 3 pages

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Wait 5 minutes. Pain should subside.
If pain does not subside
Repeat step 5.1.

5.2 Lower Injection Technique
Needle 25G →|← Syringe 10 mL
Remove needle cap and temporarily stow.
Place thumb in deepest portion of coronoid notch.
(Figure 3)

[Figure 3]

> **[Figure description]** Figure 3 illustrates the "Lower Nerve Block" procedure, divided into two panels: "Point of Insertion" and "Target Area."

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

The "Point of Insertion" panel is a photograph of a patient's open mouth. A gloved finger is placed in the "Coronoid Notch." A "Dental Syringe (Dental -2) with Long Needle (Dental 6)" is positioned for insertion at the "Seam of cheek and throat."

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

The "Target Area" panel is a medical diagram showing a cross-section of the jaw and teeth. It labels the "Coronoid Notch" where the thumb is placed and the "Inferior Alveolar Nerve" as the target for the needle. The diagram shows the needle's path of insertion and specifies that the "Barrel of syringe [is] over teeth 3 and 4 on opposite side of injection." The teeth are numbered 3, 4, 5, and 6.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

The image serves to guide the operator on the correct anatomical landmarks for needle placement and the proper orientation of the syringe barrel to reach the inferior alveolar nerve.


Figure 3. Lower Nerve Block.
Insert needle just anterior at point where seam between cheek and throat turns
upward toward maxilla.
(Figure 3)

NOTE
Barrel of syringe is kept over teeth 3 and 4 on opposite side of injection.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Insert needle until bone is contacted (and only 5 to 10 mm of needle is left
exposed.)
If no bone contact
Withdraw needle slightly, reposition needle with syringe barrel over teeth 4
and 5.

If bone is contacted and more than 5 to 10 mm of needle exposed
Reposition needle with syringe barrel over teeth 2 and 3.

Dental Procedure: Nerve Block

Gently pull back on syringe plunger and inspect chamber to ensure no blood in
chamber.
Inject 2 to 3 mL of Lidocaine (Xylocaine) directly over the root of the tooth.
Withdraw needle; recap it using one-handed technique and temporarily stow.
Wait 5 minutes. Pain should subside.
If pain does not subside
Repeat step 5.2.

Place used hardware, contaminated gloves, and packaging materials in Ziplock Bag
and discard.

Temporary Tooth Filling
Minor Treatment Pack:

TOOLS:
Digital Camera
SSC

1. PHOTO DOCUMENTING DENTAL FILLING
Photo document tooth and displaced dental filling [Digital Camera].
To view anatomic reference images of the mouth and teeth, refer to Mouth
Anatomy.
If required, refer to 1.480 D4 SETUP - MEDICAL IMAGERY, all (SODF: ISS
PTV: NIKON D4).

2. REPLACING DENTAL FILLING

2.1 Don Nitrile Gloves (select appropriate size).
Inspect tooth for debris and remove any debris found.
Have subject brush teeth to assist in any debris removal.

Dental Procedure: Temporary Dental Filling

2.2 Puncture end of Temporary Tooth Filling.
Squeeze Temporary Tooth Filling material onto finger.
Roll material into a ball.

Dental Procedure: Temporary Dental Filling

2.3 Place Temporary Tooth Filling into the tooth cavity and gently form to
tooth.
Wipe off excess with Gauze 4"x4".
Have subject firmly bite down on Temporary Tooth Filling for 2 minutes,
allowing the Temporary Tooth Filling to form to the surfaces of the tooth
and to harden.
Adjust filling by removing and smoothing excess filling using Gauze 4"x4"
and Dental Carver File (G).

Dental Procedure: Temporary Dental Filling

Filling will achieve initial set in 5 minutes.
Subject may resume drinking in approximately 1 hour.
Limit solid or hard food consumption on the affected side of the mouth for
first 12 to 24 hours as filling hardens.

NOTE
It is important to keep individuals' data separate by making separate
folders that are labeled with crewname and GMT day.

This image serves as Figure 1 to illustrate the dental

Dental Procedure: Dental Crown Replacement

image serves as Figure 1 to illustrate the dental adhesive equipment used in the dental crown replacement procedure.

Dental Procedure: Dental Crown Replacement

Figure 2. Connecting Dental Tip.

3. REPLACING DENTAL CROWN
Don Nitrile Gloves.

3.1 Preparing Loose Crown
Remove residual cement from crown and tooth utilizing Dental Carver File
(G).
Carefully check fit of crown by placing on tooth and having subject bite
down.
Remove crown and dry off with Gauze 4"x4".
Temporarily stow crown in Gauze 4"x4".

Dental Procedure: Dental Crown Replacement

3.2 Replacing Crown
Coat the surfaces of the crown that will contact tooth with Dental
Adhesive.
Place crown over tooth, ensuring proper orientation.
Place a Cotton Swab over crown and have subject gently bite down on
Cotton Swab and crown for 3 to 5 seconds to fully seat the crown.
Remove Cotton Swab and have subject bite down a second time to
determine if crown is fully seated.

Dental Procedure: Dental Crown Replacement

3.3 Confirming Crown Placement
If crown fully seated
Place Cotton Swab back over crown and have subject bite down and
apply moderate pressure for 2 to 3 minutes.

If crown not fully seated

It is important to keep individuals' data separate by making separate
folders that are labeled with crewname and GMT day.

4. PHOTO DOCUMENTING REPAIR
Photo document tooth and repaired crown [Digital Camera].
SSC
Downlink photos for Surgeon.
If required, refer to 1.480 D4 SETUP - MEDICAL IMAGERY, all (SODF: ISS
PTV: NIKON D4).

Dental Procedure: Exposed Dental Pulp

2.6.304 DENTAL PROCEDURE - EXPOSED DENTAL PULP
(MED CL/E44 - ALL/FIN 5/T)
Page 1 of 2 pages

OBJECTIVE:
To treat a fractured tooth showing exposed pulp.

ITEMS:
Ziplock Bag, 12"x12" for trash
Convenience Medication Pack (White):
Ibuprofen (Motrin)
Medical Supply Pack (Green):
Gauze 4"x4"
Nitrile Gloves (select appropriate size)
Oral Medication Pack (Purple):
Hydrocodone and Acetaminophen (Vicodin HP)
Topical & Injectable Medication Pack (Brown):
Eugenol

TOOLS:
Digital Camera
SSC

Dental Procedure: Exposed Dental Pulp

TOOLS:
Digital Camera
SSC

1. PHOTO DOCUMENTING BROKEN TOOTH

1.1 Photo document tooth and broken fragment [Digital Camera].
To view anatomic reference images of the mouth and teeth, refer to Mouth
Anatomy.

SSC
1.2 Downlink photos for Surgeon.
If required, refer to 1.480 D4 SETUP - MEDICAL IMAGERY, all (SODF:
ISS PTV: NIKON D4).

Dental Procedure: Exposed Dental Pulp

2. Take two (2) tablets Ibuprofen (Motrin) (400mg per tablet) [Convenience
Medication Pack (White)] and one (1) tablet Hydrocodone and
Acetaminophen (Vicodin HP) (10mg/300mg per tablet) [Oral Medication Pack
(Purple)] for pain control.
If required, refer to 2.0.402 TREATING PAIN, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS,
PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT).
Consult with Surgeon on type of block to place.
Perform 2.6.301 DENTAL PROCEDURE - NERVE BLOCK, all (SODF: MED
CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT), then:

Dental Procedure: Exposed Dental Pulp

3. TREATING WITH EUGENOL

WARNING

Minimize contact between the Eugenol and skin or gums to prevent tissue
damage.

3.1 Don Nitrile Gloves.
Moisten a small portion of a Gauze 4"x4" with Eugenol [Topical &
Injectable Medication Pack (Brown)].

30 JUN 15
2.6.304_M_23053.xml

2.6.304 DENTAL PROCEDURE - EXPOSED DENTAL PULP
(MED CL/E44 - ALL/FIN 5/T)
Page 2 of 2 pages

Squeeze excess Eugenol out of Gauze 4"x4" onto a second piece of
Gauze 4"x4" or a Dry Wipe.

Dental Procedure: Exposed Dental Pulp

3.2 Place moistened portion of Gauze 4"x4" over exposed pulp.
Have patient hold gauze with teeth for 1 to 2 minutes.

3.3 Additional placement of Eugenol should not require additional local
anesthesia.

4. Avoid chewing on affected side and keep food out of cavity.

Dental Procedure: Loss Of Tooth

2.6.305 DENTAL PROCEDURE - LOSS OF TOOTH
(MED CL/E44 - ALL/FIN 5/T)
Page 1 of 1 page

OBJECTIVE:
To stop bleeding and control pain.

ITEMS:
Convenience Medication Pack (White):
Ibuprofen (Motrin)
Medical Supply Pack (Green):
Gauze 4"x4"
Oral Medication Pack (Purple):
Hydrocodone and Acetaminophen (Vicodin HP)

TOOLS:
Digital Camera
SSC

1. CONTROLLING BLEEDING
Apply direct pressure to the tooth socket with Gauze 4"x4".
Hold continuous pressure until bleeding stops.

Dental Procedure: Loss Of Tooth

2. TREATING PAIN
Take two (2) tablets Ibuprofen (Motrin) (400 mg per tablet) ) [Convenience
Medication Pack (White)] and one (1) tablet Hydrocodone and
Acetaminophen (Vicodin HP) (10 mg/300 mg per tablet) [Oral Medication
Pack (Purple)] for pain control.
If required, refer to 2.0.402 TREATING PAIN, all (SODF: MED CL: EXAMS,
PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT).

Dental Procedure: Loss Of Tooth

If pain severe or immediate relief required
Perform 2.6.301 DENTAL PROCEDURE - NERVE BLOCK, all (SODF:
MED CL: EXAMS, PROCEDURES, AND TREATMENT), then:

3. Place tooth in Ziplock Bag and temporarily stow.

4. PHOTO DOCUMENTING BROKEN TOOTH

4.1 Photo document tooth and mouth [Digital Camera].
To view anatomic reference images of the mouth and teeth, refer to Mouth
Anatomy.

Dental Procedure: Loss Of Tooth

SSC
4.2 Downlink photos for Surgeon.
If required, refer to 1.480 D4 SETUP - MEDICAL IMAGERY, all (SODF:
ISS PTV: NIKON D4).

5. Discard Ziplock Bag.
